display.purpose: The homepage name only. Lowercase, tracking-tight.

body.purpose: Bio and prose. Sentence case — never lowercase prose (The Two Registers Rule).

label.purpose: Chips, captions, bylines, nav links. Lowercase, tracking-widest.

shadows.name: polaroid-float
shadows.purpose: The single true drop shadow: lifts the expanded photo off the dimmed sky.

shadows.name: moon-halo
shadows.purpose: Luminous point, not a shape — depth from light.

motion.name: feedback
motion.purpose: Hover lift, border brighten — quick, quietly eager.

motion.name: weather
motion.purpose: Sky gradient, ink, chip, glow transitions — reads as weather, not UI.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Moon face bloom, finale bursts — surfaces rather than inflates.

components.name: Sky Chip
components.description: Bordered pill on the computed 7:1 chip surface; lifts 2px on hover.
components.css: .ds-sky-chip { background: var(--card-bg, #141a3d); color: var(--chip-ink, #f1eefb); border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, currentColor 30%, transparent); border-radius: 9999px; padding: 0.375rem 0.75rem; font-family: 'Space Grotesk Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: lowercase; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 0.3s ease, border-color 0.3s ease; } .ds-sky-chip:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); border-color: color-mix(in srgb, currentColor 80%, transparent); } .ds-sky-chip:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid currentColor; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Squiggle Link
components.description: The one universal link trait: wavy 1px underline, 4px offset, lowercase, 2px hover lift.
components.css: .ds-link-squiggle { color: var(--chip-ink, #f1eefb); font-family: 'Space Grotesk Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: lowercase; text-decoration-line: underline; text-decoration-style: wavy; text-decoration-thickness: 1px; text-underline-offset: 4px; display: inline-block; transition: transform 0.3s ease; } .ds-link-squiggle:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-link-squiggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid currentColor; outline-offset: 4px; }

components.name: Sky Card
components.description: Content block sitting directly on the sky; computed chip background, 1rem radius.
components.html:
color

Everything is computed at runtime from the sun and moon position.
components.css: .ds-sky-card { background: var(--card-bg, #141a3d); color: var(--ink, #f1eefb); border-radius: 1rem; padding: 1.5rem; font-family: 'Space Grotesk Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.6; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.75rem; } .ds-sky-card-title { font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 500; text-transform: lowercase; margin: 0; }

components.name: Polaroid
components.description: The signature expanded-photo card: warm ivory, caption in the bottom margin, the system's only drop shadow.
components.css: .ds-polaroid { background: #f7f4ed; padding: 1rem 1rem 1.25rem; border-radius: 0.25rem; box-shadow: 0 25px 60px -12px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.6); display: inline-block; margin: 0; } .ds-polaroid-photo { width: 180px; height: 220px; background: linear-gradient(180deg, #1e2c60, #0a1030); border-radius: 2px; } .ds-polaroid-caption { color: #2a2620; text-align: center; font-family: 'Space Grotesk Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.9rem; letter-spacing: 0.03em; padding-top: 0.85rem; }

components.name: Icon Button
components.description: Small visible glyph, invisible ::after expands the hit area to 44px; 2px hover lift.
components.css: .ds-icon-btn { position: relative; width: 2.25rem; height: 2.25rem; color: #f1eefb; background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.12); border: none; border-radius: 9999px; font-size: 1.1rem; line-height: 1; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.3s ease; } .ds-icon-btn::after { content: ''; position: absolute; inset: -0.25rem; } .ds-icon-btn:hover { background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.22); } .ds-icon-btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f1eefb; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Position Dots
components.description: Instagram-style carousel indicator; active dot grows 1.35x and brightens with the swipe's ease-out.
components.css: .ds-dots { display: flex; justify-content: center; gap: 0.1rem; } .ds-dot { width: 1.4rem; height: 1.4rem; padding: 0; border: none; background: transparent; display: grid; place-items: center; cursor: pointer; } .ds-dot::before { content: ''; width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 9999px; background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.35); transition: background-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), transform 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-dot.is-active::before { background: #f1eefb; transform: scale(1.35); }

narrative.overview: Nothing on this site is a theme; it is weather. The background is the visitor's actual sky — sun and moon positions computed for their location, the ~70 brightest stars placed for their latitude and the hour — and every color the interface wears is derived from that sky at runtime. Colors are never picked, they are computed, with accessibility as a hard constraint. Under that sky sits a warm, lowercase, human voice: squiggle-underlined links, polaroid photo cards, jumbomoji captions, tap sparkles, a moon with a face if you know where to press. Interactive elements are quietly eager — small and unassuming at rest, but everything lifts 2px to meet your cursor.
keyCharacteristics: Sky-computed palette with build-enforced AAA contrast; fixed colors are rare, named exceptions
One typeface (Space Grotesk), two registers: lowercase chrome, sentence-case prose
Depth from light (glows, halos, the horizon gradient), not shadow
Motion at two speeds: weather (1.6–2s) and feedback (0.3s)
Delight hidden in plain sight: shooting stars, sparkles, the moon's face

rules.name: The No-Hardcode Rule
rules.body: Any color that should track the sky comes from the custom properties. A fixed hex on sky-tracking content is a bug, and check:contrast exists to catch the consequences.

rules.name: The One Warm Exception Rule
rules.body: Polaroid Ivory is the only surface allowed to ignore the sky. Everything else participates in the weather.

rules.name: The Two Registers Rule
rules.body: UI chrome is lowercase by design; body prose uses normal sentence case for readability. Don't lowercase prose to force consistency with the chrome.

rules.name: The Glow-Over-Shadow Rule
rules.body: New elements that need depth get light — a halo, a gradient toward the horizon, a brightened border — never a gray drop shadow. The polaroid keeps its float because it is a physical object.

dos: Do source every sky-tracking color from the custom properties and run pnpm check:contrast after touching sky.ts or the palette.
Do give every inline text link the squiggle underline (1px wavy, 4px offset) — no exceptions.
Do keep interactive feedback at 300ms and sky transitions at 1.6–2s; weather is slow, feedback is quick.
Do expand touch targets to 44px with invisible ::after hit areas.
Do provide a prefers-reduced-motion alternative for every animation.
Do keep chrome lowercase and prose sentence-case (The Two Registers Rule).
donts: Don't build the dev-portfolio template: hero + skills grid + project cards + testimonial slider.
Don't introduce corporate/LinkedIn energy: Title-Case professionalism, buzzwords, resume voice.
Don't ship AI-slop scaffolding: cream page backgrounds, eyebrow kickers, gradient text, generic identical card grids.
Don't tip into the over-animated showreel: no scroll-jacking, no WebGL award-bait burying the person under effects.
Don't hardcode a hex on anything meant to track the sky, and don't add gray drop shadows where a glow belongs.
Don't add a second typeface or uppercase the chrome; one family, two registers, is the voice.
